title: Ransomware Response Quick Start
description: A compact tabletop scenario for practising first-hour ransomware decisions.

injects.title: Multiple Workstations Locked
injects.content: Service desk reports encrypted files and ransom notes on several finance workstations. Shared drives are intermittently unavailable.

options.label: Isolate affected systems and preserve evidence
Keep monitoring while collecting more detail

injects.title: Containment Window
injects.content: Network telemetry suggests the ransomware has not reached production servers. The business wants a recovery estimate before noon.

options.label: Start restore validation from offline backups
Delay recovery until forensics are complete

injects.title: Lateral Movement Confirmed
injects.content: Additional servers begin showing encryption activity. The attacker claims to have copied customer records.

options.label: Move to crisis footing and notify legal

injects.title: Notification Decision
injects.content: Legal asks whether the available evidence meets the threshold for regulator notification. Communications needs approved holding lines.

triggers_communications.subject: Request for comment
triggers_communications.body: We are hearing reports of a cyber incident affecting your customer portal. Can you confirm?

injects.title: Business Pressure Builds
injects.content: Finance cannot process payments. The CEO asks whether paying the ransom would speed recovery.
expected_actions: Compare payment risk against recovery options

debrief_notes: Focus debrief on incident declaration, containment speed, notification thresholds, and executive decision quality.
